utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, product-specific onboarding redesign plans that passed all 7 evaluation criteria. Both correctly identified 'invite a teammate' as the primary activation event grounded in cohort data, called out the 7-step wizard as the primary bottleneck, recommended a product-first flow pattern with context justification, provided prioritized checklists with microcopy, delivered concrete empty state copy, designed behavior-triggered email sequences, and specified exact funnel metrics. The wi_skills output (17,098 bytes) is larger and more detailed than wo_skills (13,867 bytes), with richer step-by-step flow descriptions, more explicit behavioral justifications, and a more structured framework applied throughout. items.criterion: The strategy must explicitly target getting users to complete 3 workouts within 7 days as the activation threshold, and design a habit loop (trigger, action, reward, investment) that specifically serves this goal. The loop must name a concrete trigger (e.g., time-based or behavior-based), a specific reward tied to completion, and an investment step that increases switching cost. A strategy that only recommends 'add streaks' or 'send reminders' without articulating a loop structure grounded in the 3-workout threshold fails. Pass: a named loop structure is present with all four components tied to the retention data.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive 30-day engagement strategies that passed all verifier checks and satisfied all seven judge evaluation items. Both correctly identified the 3-workout/7-day activation threshold, designed habit loops, redesigned the post-workout screen with specific next-step CTAs, proposed behavior-triggered notification strategies, defined milestone celebrations, specified stalled-user recovery protocols, and included structured A/B test hypotheses. The wi_skills output is marginally more structured and specific — it provides more granular copy direction (exact headline text, distinct CTA hierarchy), cleaner section organization, and a more explicit habit-loop framing tied directly to the onboarding-cro skill framework. However, the wo_skills output is also strong and substantive, demonstrating that this particular baseline agent was capable of producing a near-equivalent output without the skill. The practical quality difference is small; both would be actionable for product and engineering teams. wi_skills edges ahead on structural rigor and specificity of copy direction.

The key differentiators are J4 and J5. For J4 (guided tour/tooltip specification), wi_skills leveraged the skill framework to produce specific tooltip placements with copy text and trigger logic for at least 2 UI touchpoints, while wo_skills only gave generic structural advice about adding tooltips.
